reviews.review_msg: The basic concepts of this course are easy, but Mandana sometimes complicates them. Attendance is mandatory. If this is a required course, make sure to remember everything, focus on what she says, and apply it (she'll test this). Having an appointment with her might help, as she can give you some advice to improve your studying and presentation.

She is so sweet. I don't understand how students can hate her, I think it is possible only if they are lazy and do not want to do anything and get good grades. Very caring, and helpful. She also replies to emails quickly. If it was possible I would take every course with her!

I with my friend have finished 2 courses with Prof. Mandana this semester. It were the most interesting courses we have ever taken here. In my opinion it is one of the best professors you can find here. 
(But be ready to participate in classes, it is real matters)

This semester I took 2 courses(BPK140 and HSCI160) with Mandana, and do not understand these reviews and the low ratings. She is an amazing prof, her slides are clear, and her explanation is as well. She is always welcome to answer questions either during the class or after. Courses are interesting and a bit difficult but you will learn a lot.
Avoid. Doesnt care
Easy class but uncaring and hard to deal with if you have problems. Pick another Prof or prepare for stress if you run into problems.

She is a wonderful presenter but a horrible teacher. She does not specify or highlight what is important. There is an abundance of information and the class dropped my GPA when I took it as a GPA booster. I am disappointed and I generally do well in my classes. I: The rest of the class didn't seem to do very well, average was fairly low.
She is the best prof ever! She is strict and yes, she should be. People especially women feel jealous at her because she is beautiful and smart.

Mandana was a really nice prof and everything. However, her exams were quite difficult if you don't pay attention to everything she says in lecture. Quizzes every tutorial which were helpful to know what you were learning.

Had a good sense of humour, but came across as a little condescending to students. Mandatory tutorial as there is a quiz every week. Liked to stick to the textbook but everything was straightforward and overall a fair class.

In general she made rude comments, did not answer questions, cut students off and was very good at making students feel stupid. She copies straight from the textbook and is very picky with her marking especially about spelling. There is a quiz every week so attending tutorials is mandatory if not you will receive a zero for that quiz.

In general she makes rude remarks,talks over you, doesn't answer your questions and is very good at making students feel stupid. she is very picky; marks for detail and spelling even if you miss an E at the end of a word you get the answer incorrect. She copies straight from the text, there is a quiz every week so you must attend all tutorials.

The basic concepts of this course are easy, but Mandana sometimes complicates them. Attendance is mandatory. If this is a required course, make sure to remember everything, focus on what she says, and apply it (she'll test this). Having an appointment with her might help, as she can give you some advice to improve your studying and presentation.

She is so sweet. I don't understand how students can hate her, I think it is possible only if they are lazy and do not want to do anything and get good grades. Very caring, and helpful. She also replies to emails quickly. If it was possible I would take every course with her!

I with my friend have finished 2 courses with Prof. Mandana this semester. It were the most interesting courses we have ever taken here. In my opinion it is one of the best professors you can find here. 
(But be ready to participate in classes, it is real matters)

This semester I took 2 courses(BPK140 and HSCI160) with Mandana, and do not understand these reviews and the low ratings. She is an amazing prof, her slides are clear, and her explanation is as well. She is always welcome to answer questions either during the class or after. Courses are interesting and a bit difficult but you will learn a lot.
Avoid. Doesnt care
Easy class but uncaring and hard to deal with if you have problems. Pick another Prof or prepare for stress if you run into problems.

She is a wonderful presenter but a horrible teacher. She does not specify or highlight what is important. There is an abundance of information and the class dropped my GPA when I took it as a GPA booster. I am disappointed and I generally do well in my classes. I: The rest of the class didn't seem to do very well, average was fairly low.
She is the best prof ever! She is strict and yes, she should be. People especially women feel jealous at her because she is beautiful and smart.

Mandana was a really nice prof and everything. However, her exams were quite difficult if you don't pay attention to everything she says in lecture. Quizzes every tutorial which were helpful to know what you were learning.

Had a good sense of humour, but came across as a little condescending to students. Mandatory tutorial as there is a quiz every week. Liked to stick to the textbook but everything was straightforward and overall a fair class.

In general she made rude comments, did not answer questions, cut students off and was very good at making students feel stupid. She copies straight from the textbook and is very picky with her marking especially about spelling. There is a quiz every week so attending tutorials is mandatory if not you will receive a zero for that quiz.

In general she makes rude remarks,talks over you, doesn't answer your questions and is very good at making students feel stupid. she is very picky; marks for detail and spelling even if you miss an E at the end of a word you get the answer incorrect. She copies straight from the text, there is a quiz every week so you must attend all tutorials.

very easy class, attend all lectures to complete participation activities which are free marks, also there are weekly online participation activities that are free marks if you do them. Exams consisted of MC, TF, fill in the blank, and short answers. Everything was easy but fill in the blank was a little tricky. Overall very easy to get high mark

very easy class, attend all lectures to complete participation activities which are free marks, also there are weekly online participation activities that are free marks if you do them. Exams consisted of MC, TF, fill in the blank, and short answers. Everything was easy but fill in the blank was a little tricky. Overall very easy to get high mark
Mike is a good teacher! He is very knowledgeable on the subject. He does not require a textbook for the course and he gives you all his notes. I would recommend him.
Mike is a good teacher! He is very knowledgeable on the subject. He does not require a textbook for the course and he gives you all his notes. I would recommend him.

Weekly quizzes were not bad but they do not reflect the types of questions on exams. Exam qs were not what I was expecting just based of the material we were given and were rather confusing to answer. Lecture videos were of other profs so I'm not sure if she even knew what we were learning. She's just the supervisor for the course not the prof

Gave a ridiculous amount of reading for the whole course, sometimes assigning 2 chapters per week (17 chapters of reading for 12 units/modules). Tests were often way too detailed and contained very specific questions about little facts sprinkled throughout the text. Majority of test questions are short-answer, so much writing is needed.

Super frustrating. The prof would post old and outdated lectures. Quizzes were okay but the final essay was graded unfairly. Wasn't until the middle of the semester that she told us we had to post our discussion posts at the beginning of the week instead of the actual due date in order to get full marks, so the grading was really unclear.

I took BPK 140 with Prof. Blabber. When I went to ask him something, he looked me up and down in a very uncomfortable way. I wasn't even wearing anything revealing. I'm wondering if anyone has had similar experiences, as I got very off vibes.

worst prof ever dont take this class with him hes such an unfair marker his exam questions are things we didnt even cover and they are so specific and attendance counts but all he does is repeat his slides the whole time

Dr Blaber is a great prof, bpk 140 as my elective was an easy class (as a business kid) and he cares alot about what he teaches. Not sure why he gets so much stick on rate my prof, I would take a class from him again.

If you took Bio12 this course is super easy, discussions are marked by the TA and it is easy to get a good mark. He does quizzes in his lectures so you do have to attend, and quizzes were online and pretty easy. He's a good prof and tries to make lectures interesting, I would recommend for breadth requirements.

Dr. Blaber is kind, and extremely knowledgeable, but his course was very poorly designed and way too much work for a 100 level course. So many weekly assignments including difficult discussions, and very unclear and poorly written quizzes with info mentioned only once out of 60 pages of reading and NOT on the slides; unable to reach out of class.

As a business student, I enjoyed his class. It was interesting material but he was a bit dry. Testing is fair and no curveballs. Study the material and you can get a good mark. Hes pretty  fair with his assignments and tests and doesnt pile on the work unnecessarily. No group projects which is a bonus.
First-level course with very high amount of content, quite difficult quizzes and tests. Don't take BPK 140 with him as an elective.

Take this course online if you want the best grade possible, the midterm was fairly easy, the final was somewhat tougher but worth 36% of your mark, tons of tough quizes for a first year course, TA(Meihui) had horrible English and was really difficult to understand, she also graded written answers very harshly, I worked so hard just to get a B...

We had weekly quizzes in the lecture+tutorial, and the tutorial quizzes (made by the TA) often had failing averages. Although they're not worth much, it adds up. Besides the quizzes, a lot of the tasks worth marks are easy. The midterm seemed easier than the final, but they were mostly fair. Attendance matters because you need a code for quizzes.

Dr. Blaber was an alright prof at best. His lectures were somewhat interesting at times. I found him quite difficult to reach, as he doesn't respond to emails/Canvas messages even after multiple follow-ups, and the TA's are hit or miss. Marking was tough and a bit unfair.

mainly exams are based off of lec. quizzes are off text. Proff Blaber is truly a good person and good proff. however, the ta is what makes this class (bpk 140) a pain. Meuheu marks way too harsh and is sometimes inconsiderate. but, she is open to discussion but that is a 50/50 chance of a grade change

Assigns a lot of readings but they are straightforward and he summarizes them in the lectures, which are entertaining. Professor Blaber is kind, but the TA is rude and unhelpful. His exam questions are not difficult but they were marked harshly by the TA.

Your grade is based of a large amount of small assignments so having one bad day won't ruin your ability to get a good grade. In the tutorials you are given hints about future assignments. Professor Blaber is kind and wants his students to do well.

Overall Dr. Blazer is a rather boring professor despite his best efforts. The class has alot of material and reading which can make it overwhelming. The midterm was graded quite harshly but grading was fair overall. Dr. Blaber was quite kind and responsive the few times I reached out to him.
He is a very solid professor. Most important thing in this class is to study for pop quizzes and take notes of the lecture slides.

Make sure you study for the weekly quizzes they're easy if you study for them. Don't need to attend lecture, but keep up with the course material. Assignments are pretty easy. There is a lot of the info but so much of it is common knowledge, definitely some interesting material as well. The grading was a bit tough for the short answers on exams.

While some of the comments below are true, such as heavy reading, difficult for a level 100 course, Professor Blaber is really quite considerate. Reading the textbook as well as the required weekly readings are SUPER important, even for weekly quizzes and finals. However, I did not mind the 8:30am lectures, as he actually made it interesting.
This guy is stronger then melatonin.

Wow, way to make an interesting class so dry and hard. Never have I hated a class so much. Literally so much wrong with the layout of his class, I thought I'd give it a try as it was the only class to fit with my schedule. JUST DONT!!!

This is a 100 level course but the way he made it looked like it was a 4 credit course. There are so many reading, weekly quizzes, 4 of 500 word discussion, weekly assignments and his exam doesn't only test on lecture material, he also tests on the textbook, the assignments and the articles provided. This was such a waste of time.

Dr. Blaber's exams are fairly straightforward, just read the questions carefully. Also, study the weekly topics using the textbook, and memorize key definitions. The TAs mark your assignments, which weren't too difficult.

He does not give enough time during exams to actually finish the questions. It was not necessary a hard class, but he was not a good teacher. He made things unnecessarily confusing and would do stuff to, I SWEAR, mess up students when they were taking tests/doing assignments. the whole structure of his course was also super confusing.

Amanda was a good professor. Not sure why her ratings are so low. Her lectures were interesting and  easy to understand. A lot of content to remember but overall manageable if you pay attention in class, take notes, and study a little bit each week. Midterm was hard but was able to redeem myself on the final which was surprisingly easier.

I'm pretty sure the first rating for this prof was written by the prof herself. She seems like a nice person with a lot of knowledge in health but this class is insanely hard for a 100 level course. Midterm was very difficult and covered things that weren't mentioned in lecture. You would have to memorize the entire textbook to get a good grade.

Ain't no way the prof wrote her own review lmao. Amanda you're awful stick
to Physiotherapy. There's a reason we had like the lowest average compared to recent semesters. Even our TA was confused with some of your explanations.

Amanda was a good prof, lectures were not too bad but boring at times. The midterm exam was unreasonable and focused on oddly specific details rather than practical knowledge. Tutorials helped a lot for the written part of exams. I am also pretty sure the prof herself wrote the good review.

I regret taking this course as a B-Science elective. While the content is informative, lectures are unengaging, as the professor reads from slides. Exams are overly detailed, focusing on statistics rather than practical knowledge. However, the professor is caring and open to questions, showing potential for improvement.

Amanda is the best! Her lectures are to the point and her slides are helpful, there is a textbook that is optional but if you can find it just studying the learning objectives listed on every lecture will help you do great on exams. She is very friendly and has great clinical anecdotes from her physiotherapist job and atheletic background.

Not sure why he has such low ratings. You can tell that he tries to be entertaining for the students by making jokes, showing YouTube science parodies etc. though they don't usually get the response I'd assume he's hoping for. I found his lectures interesting and honestly really enjoyed the course despite not being in science.

It has an 8:30am required attendance + in class quizzes. And also quizzes in the tutorials on the previous weeks material. Unless you're putting 100% effort into this class do not take it as an elective. Prof was often behind on lectures and skipped slides. The TA (Vardhan) was nice, he wasn't harsh on marks, and made muffins for the last tutorial.

The content itself, the assgns, quizzes, exams, are not hard. But, it's an annoying amount of work, specially for a 100 level course. The "attendance check" quizzes he does for an 8:30 class, on top of his kinda boring lectures like... I would not take this class with him. The TA (Vardhan) was quite nice though.

Lectures were always behind and tedious. Quizzes in class are easy, but I don't understand why they make you write another quiz in tutorials, even though each lecture is a week behind. TA is also nasty. She was always rude towards her students. Every single day, she wakes up and chooses violence.
Makes interesting material super boring. The class is very writing heavy and research based. He expects you to read a lot of papers for assignments and tutorials. Rambles a lot in lectures
the content was very interesting however lectures were a bit boring at times. the weekly quizzes were difficult but didn't count too much towards grade. overall an average class

Not sure what people are talking about here. As a business student, I enjoyed his class. It was interesting material but he was a bit dry. Testing is fair and no curveballs. Study the material and you can get a good mark. Hes pretty fair with his assignments and tests and doesnt pile on the work unnecessarily. No group projects which is a bonus.
This prof literally wants to gatekeep GPAs and makes a very easy course extra tricky and hard. In addition, the lectures were very boring. Do not recommend him, makes BPK 140 boring.

DO NOT TAKE HIS CLASS, my friend had taken BPK 140 in the past and said it was easy and a GPA booster, BUT THIS MANZ IS TERRIBLE. he is a super hard grader and makes lectures soo dry. Like I want to be nice to him, but i just cant.

He literally wants you to fail. Worst teacher I have ever had. Makes things unnecessarily hard and tests you on irrelevant and super minute things so it makes studying a nightmare. His exams are not given enough time and most of the students arent able to finish on time. He makes interesting this suuuper boring. Overall do not recommend.

Makes the interesting material extremely boring and makes every questions a trick and confusing. Memorize everything from every slide because he will quiz and test you on the most random and pointless parts of the material.
Great guy tbh. Class isnt easy but it is very easy to follow and hard to get left behind.

Good god man this whole class was just propaganda about how meat is awful and vegetarianism is the key to life. He makes you buy HIS OWN TEXTBOOK to study for the exam and has the worst lecture slides you'll probably ever see. Completely disregards the impact of physical activity on health. I thought these reviews were harsh, they really are not
avoid at all costs, worst professor I've encountered, made a very interesting class very boring. very poor lectures. I did well in this class by ignoring his lectures and only using the textbook.

Not as bad as I thought he would be. You really have to pay attention in lectures though because he'll use random things he said in lecture on the tests. The tests weren't too bad, but the quizzes were.
The exams felt fair to me but the quizzes were difficult. I liked mike as a professor and he was entertaining and relatable.

Very disorganized prof, docs don't match his hour long slides at all. Things he says don't match the docs or the slides. it's like 3 lectures combined into one. BPK140 is interesting, but taking it with any other prof would be more beneficial. He also brags about himself a lot, really think he should just retire at this point

First BPK140 is the most underrated course. A course content that should have been mandatory during high school. The truth is many people don't take the time to learn about modern health and managing your wellness. This course provides insights into these topics and will definitively increase your awareness to make better decisions in life.

I was scared prior to taking his course because of his ratings, but he's not that crazy bad except for the fact that he didn't give any lectures. The weekly quizzes were fairly easy but the midterm and the final exam were hard. I didn't use the textbook and still got a B, but if you want an A, you probably should read the textbook.

Mike is not very accessible when it comes to Office Hours and is disorganized. Would not take him again and TA's are hard markers. Marking criteria is also very weird and the curve will mess with your grade.

Worst professor I've had at SFU and I've been there for five years. Completely unprofessional, did not give one lecture (all lectures were pre recorded by other faculty members) thus the only contact I had with him was during the midterm and final exam. The course was extremely disorganized, no clear assignment instructions, unclear deadlines etc.
Mans makes up rules on the spot really does not care about students

Seemed nice, but was very disorganized, usually didnt know the answer to Q's & did little to help. Quizzes weekly, but they all varied from time of day & time limit, so the class did not have much structure. We were not given enough time for the final exam and the last Q was worth HALF (50 marks) of the entire final (no warning of format before).

Very nice prof. Cares a lot about if his students understand. Always available for questions after/before class if needed. There is a small lecture quiz every class tho so be prepared to read, though it is very easy. Midterms were pretty easy with very short essay questions and fill in the blanks. Would 10/10 take again. Lenient marking.

My least favourite prof, exams are fill in the blanks and short answer, questions can be unfair and he isn't helpful. Difficult to talk too. He is passionate about what he teaches, but I would not take a class again with him as it feels like Im just memorizing his notes. he does quizzes in lecture and tutorial, every single one.

great guy, really cares about his students. His exams are really fair and if you read the word doc notes he posts you should do great! the projects are fun and final was very fair as well if you study hard you will do great!
Good professor. Pretty simple if you read the material.

Mike is blunt but highly humorousnice prof.  He provides notes, and only test from them. Unfortunately no one seems to visit him during office hours. (Please do he seems lonely and will welcome you :). I sent in some questions for the midterm he actually used a couple, definitely aced those! Lovely B-sci-elective.

Mike is a great prof. If you show up to class, you will do fine on the midterms. The tutorial isn't for marks, and is somewhat pointless, but the class is extremely interesting and is a great science requirement.

Material ranged from dry- interesting. the tests focused on very specific details like random percentages and memorizing certain words for fill in the blanks. Lots of math errors on tests in terms of marking, always double check your mark.  What kind of prof has a "midterm" on the very last class? thats just cruel. Wouldn't take his class again.

Don't know why Mike has such a bad rating. Straight forward prof, exams are straight from the notes and are very easy to get a good grade in if you put it the time to study. His humour is subtle but enjoyable. Would definitely recommend taking BPK with Mike!  Side note: this is a great class to take if you need a science requirement.

His lectures are very boring and not engaging. Less that half of the students showed up for lectures. I took this class as an easy elective, but turns out his exams are brutal. The TA only marks directly off the answer key, and questions on the exams were very difficult to understand. Overall bad prof, avoid taking classes with him.

This class is really easy if you are an Arts student wanting to take a science class for your requirements. Dr. Walsh is boring, he just reads off his power points you don't even need to go to class. Tutorial is so lame but mandatory. You have a DEBATE and presentations. NO. His tests are so lame too. Avoid him if you can.

One of the best profs at SFU! Never heard anyone complain about any of her courses. Very interactive and the tests are easy. The study material is very organized and one can easily get A's if pay attention in class. Super understanding. I'm still in touch with her 2 years after taking the course. Fav prof. of all time!

Diana is an amazing prof. She is very knowledgeable about the content and is always willing to help her students. Her midterm and final exam were fair and if you study and do the readings, you should do well in her course
pick any other prof

I was so excited to take a course with Dr Bedoya after having read these amazing reviews. She is definitely a great person but man she is just so inaccessible. don't expect any email replies or replies on discussion boards... It feels as though because she did a good job setting up an online course, she said, that's it my job is done and peaced out
she is a very nice professor. explains every concept very clearly.
The final exam was quite heavy. Recommended to take this class if you are good at memorizing.

Loved this prof. She is a great teacher and her lectures/slides are clear with useful study questions. Tests are straight forward as you know exactly what youre studying for. I wish she taught all my classes!

I love Diana and I also love this course. But the tests were garbage. Expects a lot from us, we need to write super detailed in order to get a good mark. TA was kind of rude if you go to her. Need to memorize everything but I'm a English learner it's super hard. Take it if you are good at memorizing. Lectures are fun tho.

She was absolutely amazing. She goes over all the specifics and details so even if you have no science background, you still understand. She's really cheerful, especially for an 8:30 am class. Just all around, a good prof and I would take this class again with her!

I took this course because I needed a science credit, and I'm so glad I did. Diana is my favorite prof, she really cares about her students well being and she is also hilarious. Made 8:30 lectures really enjoyable. She relates material back to her own experiences and explains things really well. Would take every class with her if I could!

Diana is super passionate about what she teaches. I like that she draws experience from her own life to make it more relatable and memorable for her students. She answers questions thoroughly, and really wants her students to succeed. She is inspirational too and her lectures are very interesting!

Diana is a really inspiring professor and you can really tell she cares about the stuff she lectures about. Although you do more work and have to study a bit more than in Peter Reuben's BPK 140 class, it is still a really easy class and a great science credit for people who need one and for people who aren't science majors.
Diana is an awesome prof, it is obvious that she loves teaching. However, she doesn't reply students' email...

Heavy focus on whatever is on her lecture slides and supplementary material from the textbook. Material on lecture slides were not enough to get full marks on exams and had to have explanations from the textbook to get the full mark. If it's a list of things it'll probably on exams. Great prof but not worth the 8:30 class, Just take online

Interesting and knowledgable person and very kind over all but found course too heavily based on pure memorization. No thought needed for anything just answer what she tells you. Tests literally made us list out every point on the slides. But if u got photographic memory, this is the course for u

Diana is hands down my favourite prof so far. She's so caring, inspirational, and motivating with her lectures. The two hours don't aren't boring and dry in comparison to other classes I've taken. I looked up to her all semester and I think she's an amazing person in general. The class content was quite easy to understand too, and exams were fair!
Clear Lecture, Amazing Lecture, Hilarious

DIANA IS WONDERFUL, I TOOK BPK140 WITH HER AND IT WAS REALLY INTERESTING. I WOULD RECOMMEND HER CLASS FOR ANYONE. SHE INSPIRES YOU A LOT AND SHE MAKES HER TEACHING CRYSTAL CLEAR. SHE IS INDEED WONDERFUL. I LOVE HER AND WOULD SURELY WANT TO TAKE A COURSE WITH HER AGAIN.

Diana is a very engaging lecturer and is very friendly! She was always smiling and is definitely a role model. The class was very interesting but you have to really study for the final. The TA was horrible.

She is very knowledgable. Makes lectures enjoyable. However, it's difficult to take what she says in lecture and apply it to exams. She lectures the way we talk on a daily basis, or what we hear in the mediasurroundings everyday. Exams are very tough. TA wasn't helpful, never replied emails.

Found her teaching style super condescending, and the course material relating to social stuff suuuper normative and alienating. Not a tough class for a science credit but if you have any kind of social justice framework or are a queer, etc. you might find this class infuriating.
She is so helpful and great prof. Btw, she is so hotttt!!!

This course is suitable for those who are good at memorizing. There are plenty to memorize. If you're not good at it, Exams are tough DO NOT TAKE THIS COURSE. Yes Diana is a great professor as much as a great marking killer also.
Very good, clear, definitely knowleadgable on what she teaches

Overall a very interesting prof. Shes good at explaining and keeping you interested during lectures. The midterm was fairly easy as long as you study her study questions. The final was pretty hard though, some of the questions were not from her notes or in her study questions. The weekly quizzes were annoying and the TA was very picky.

Stephen Brown constructed the course: The videos were informative and provided the core points in the chapter and really explained quite well and enjoyable to listen and learn. The quizzes and exams I found the most challenging. My downfall in the course. A lot tougher than I hoped this class would be. But very interesting information!

Class was taken with Anne-Kristine Arnold but Stephen Brown created the course and the weekly videos were of him. Very well-organized course. Enjoyed the readings (mandatory, but entertaining - written by him); the assignments were doable; the quizzes/exams were completely fair. Not a heavy workload at all! Took as a B-SCI and loved it.

I didn't specifically have him as my professor for this class online but everything on canvas was by him including short video lecture material. In the short 10-15 minutes of him lecturing, you can thoroughly tell that he is passionate and does not bore you! Found this class very interesting and was not bored when studying. Marking depends on TA

Good prof to have for an online class. Weekly quizzes, 3 quizzes, 1 MT, 1 final all semester long. He explained his topic well in the book he wrote and the weekly videos. Exams were fair, took this as my elective. Solid class.

Tony went through the chapters WAY TOO FAST. There were so many points to be discussed. I understand its my responsibility but theres about 20 long butt chapters to memorize already, if he could just stick to his points and explain with clarity that would help me pass this course. I doubt I will be. Hes a nice guy though
My favourite Prof!! It was a super easy class, all midterms and finals were open note so we could bring anything that was handwritten and it was super interesting! AMAZING PROF loved him!

Peter is great. You can tell he genuinely cares about his students. He is a good lecturer but honestly, you do not need to attend class. I went to 2 tutorials and lots of the lectures and finished with an A. Exams are open-note but you need to make sure you write down everything that's on the slides as his questions are very specific

One of the best profs I had at SFU :) Lectures are chill and he truly cares for his students and wants everyone to do well. Highly respected by students and always willing to interact with them. Fair, funny and and approachable! I would highly recommend his BPK 140 class for anyone regardless of their major.

Peter is charismatic and passionate. He is fair to his students and makes their life easier and less stressful. He really does care! I took his BPK 140 class this winter (2016) and loved it. I recommend this class as it is relevant and interesting. The course material is not difficult. The only downside was tophat sucks and brought my grade down.
Honestly probably my favourite class by far! All of the exams were open book and they were fair. He is a very approachable and caring prof; definitely would love to take another class with him!

Penny is a very organized, mature, and intelligent prof. She speaks eloquently and teaches content well. Exams were at a reasonable difficulty; she is a strict but fair marker. If you put in the effort, you will do well in the course.
Took BPK/KIN 140 with him in Summer 2015, he is a great prof and this course is fun and easy with him.
